commit.message:
PRD: Phase 2 — Wire generate-playbook into the build (#124)

* chore: PRD: Phase 2 — Wire generate-playbook into the build (closes #118)

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #124

- _discover_overlay_variables: handle "var | default(false)" pattern
  so laptop overlay variable is correctly discovered
- _generate_host_vars_json_template: emit quoted dict keys for valid
  Jinja2 ({"bluetooth": bluetooth} not {bluetooth: bluetooth})
- write_playbook: deep copy _SECTION_DEFINITIONS to prevent mutation
  across multiple calls
- write_playbook: use profile-defined tags from role_cond.tags instead
  of deriving from role name (preserves [fonts] tag on nerd-fonts)
- write_playbook: emit double-quoted tags for Makefile grep compat
- Remove duplicate _cmd_generate_playbook function and duplicate
  argparse registration
- Update generate-playbook tests for file-write mode
- Regenerate play.yml with fixed generator

* fix: correct Jinja2 combine() pattern in host_vars_json template

Move the 'if cond else {}' inside combine() to prevent data loss
when later overlay variables are not defined. The pattern:
  | combine({...}) if cond else {}
could discard all previously accumulated vars when 'cond' is False.
The correct pattern is:
  | combine({...} if cond else {})

Fix applied in both _generate_host_vars_json_template() and play.yml.

- emitToolLifecycle: serialize under sidefxMu to prevent concurrent hook invocations
- persistedSessionFromSession: use CopyMessages/CopyState/CopyMetadata for safe snapshots
- Migrate all session metadata/state access to use new accessor methods

commit.message:
Restrict named capture group names to ASCII letters/digits (#130)

Match java.util.regex.Pattern rules: first character must be an ASCII
letter (A-Z, a-z), subsequent characters must be ASCII letters or
digits (A-Z, a-z, 0-9). Previously SafeRE was too lenient, accepting
underscores, Unicode letters/digits, and connector punctuation, and
also allowing names to start with digits.

Fixes #129

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/suasuasuasuasua/dotfiles/sessions/3995f218-1e3c-4d38-99d1-fe7e997f265e

Co-authored-by: suasuasuasuasua <72476123+suasuasuasuasua@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(nvim): only load view once per buffer using view_activated flag

The previous implementation called loadview on every BufWinEnter,
causing repeated disk reads each time you switched between windows.
Now uses a per-buffer view_activated flag (AstroNvim-style) so:
- loadview runs only once per buffer, on first entry
- filetype check ensures treesitter has set up folding before restoring
- mkview is only called when the view has been activated, avoiding
  overwriting views for buffers that were never loaded
- BufWritePost and WinLeave added as additional save triggers

commit.message:
fix(acp-log): suppress consecutive chunk frames via envelope.skip

Consecutive agent_message_chunk / agent_thought_chunk notifications
flood the ACP debug log. Only the first frame of each consecutive
same-kind + same-direction run is logged now.

Design:
- acpLogEnvelope gains updateKind (set by filterNotifySessionUpdateBody)
  and skip bool (set by the sink before deciding to log)
- acpLastRecord struct holds the last-recorded updateKind+dir pair
- defaultACPProcessLogSink is a pointer receiver with a mutex and a
  lastRecord field
- applyChunkFilter(dir, *envelope) is a focused helper: if the envelope
  is a chunk kind and matches lastRecord, it sets skip=true; otherwise
  it updates lastRecord (and resets it for non-chunk frames)
- Frame() builds the envelope, calls applyChunkFilter, returns early if
  envelope.skip is true
- filterACPBody / filterNotifySessionUpdateBody return ([]byte, string)
  to surface updateKind without a second JSON parse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(acp-log): cleaner chunk suppression via envelope.skip + lastRecord

Replace ad-hoc if/else chain in Frame() with a proper design:
- acpLogEnvelope gains a skip bool field
- acpLastRecord struct holds the last logged {updateKind, dir}
- defaultACPProcessLogSink holds lastRecord (replaces two loose fields)
- applyChunkFilter(dir, envelope) sets envelope.skip and updates lastRecord;
  non-chunk frames always reset lastRecord so the next chunk run logs again
- Frame() builds envelope, calls applyChunkFilter, returns early if skip

commit.message:
scheduler: skip redundant Telegram delivery for agent-run jobs

When provider is not 'session' (claude/copilot/codex), the agent itself
handles Telegram messaging as part of its task prompt. The scheduler was
sending the full result_text again, causing duplicate reports.

Now agent-run jobs set agent_handles_telegram=True which suppresses the
scheduler-level Telegram send on success. Result is still stored in DB.

feat: jarv CLI — install/proxy/lifecycle/update from terminal

Full CLI tool with 'jarv' prefix for VPS/headless usage:

Commands:
  jarv install         Interactive wizard (docker or native mode)
  jarv start/stop/restart/status/logs   Daemon lifecycle
  jarv update          Update Jarvis (jarvis update || bun fallback)
  jarv proxy           Cloudflare DNS + nginx + certbot SSL (interactive prompts for missing args)
  jarv help / version  Usage and version info

New files:
  cli/utils.ts                colors, exec helpers, readline, profile I/O
  cli/jarv.ts                 shebang entry point + command router
  cli/commands/install.ts     install wizard
  cli/commands/lifecycle.ts   start/stop/restart/status/logs
  cli/commands/update.ts      update logic
  cli/commands/proxy.ts       reverse proxy setup (CF + nginx + certbot)
  tsconfig.cli.json           compiles cli/ -> dist-cli/ (CommonJS)
  install-cli.sh              curl | bash one-liner installer
  CLI.md                      full CLI documentation

package.json:
  - bin.jarv -> ./dist-cli/jarv.js (npm/bun install -g support)
  - build:cli script
  - build:all script
  - version bumped to 1.2.0

Install via:
  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Yperbu9474/jarvis-installer-unofficial/main/install-cli.sh | bash

perf(maglev): optimistic Phi seeding for smi_guarded propagation

Break circular dependency in smi_guarded analysis for loop-carried
Smi values (e.g. sum = sum + obj.a).  Previously, GenericAdd needed
the Phi to be in the set, but the Phi needed GenericAdd — deadlock.

Fix: optimistically seed Phis whose preheader input is already
smi_guarded and whose consumers are all arithmetic-compatible.
The fixed-point then derives GenericAdd results, confirming the Phi.
A verification pass removes any unconfirmed optimistic Phis.

Also fix: formatting (cargo fmt), ignore release-only Maglev mutual
recursion boolean bug (test_tail_call_mutual_recursion_sloppy_small).

commit.message:
fix: recharacterize ×5.0 as calibration mismatch, not bug, in glycogen report

The demand-diagnosis-glycogen report incorrectly called the ×5.0 multiplier
a 'time-integration bug' and 'double integration'. The companion report
(demand-calibration-fix-impact) confirmed:
  - ×5.0 is dimensionally correct (U/min × 5min = U/step)
  - Removing ×5.0 worsens R² (−0.47 → −0.71, EXP-1631)
  - The actual fix is patient-specific calibration β

Additional fixes:
  - Patient a actual dBG: −0.03 → +0.12 mg/dL/step (sign and magnitude)
  - '10/11 monotonic' → '10/11 increasing trend (7 strictly monotonic)'
  - 'β doubles' → 'β more than doubles (2.6×)' (0.273→0.715)
  - Updated stale line references (108→158, 544→546, 454→455)
  - Updated §4 heading and §7 recommendations to reflect implemented fix
  - Updated appendix A7 assumption

fix: resolve all CI failures from Cloudflare integration

Cloud Armor (CEL expression limit exceeded):
- Replaced single-CEL deny rule (22 IPs > 5-expression limit) with three
  batched SRC_IPS_V1 ALLOW rules (priorities 100/101/102, ≤10 ranges each)
  plus a catch-all DENY at priority 200 when Cloudflare is enabled
- Rate-limit and WAF rules are now dynamic and only added when Cloudflare is
  NOT enabled (Cloudflare handles DDoS + WAF at the edge)

TLS (Origin CA API permission error):
- Removed cloudflare_origin_ca_certificate + tls_cert_request (requires
  Zone:SSL and Certificates permission not present on the token)
- Replaced with tls_self_signed_cert — sufficient because Cloudflare 'Full'
  SSL mode accepts self-signed origin certs and Cloud Armor blocks all
  non-Cloudflare IPs from reaching the origin directly
- Cloudflare zone SSL setting changed from 'strict' to 'full' accordingly
- Added removed{} block for google_compute_managed_ssl_certificate.ui so
  OpenTofu drops it from state without destroying it in GCP (the old cert
  osrs-ui-cert is no longer referenced by the HTTPS proxy after this apply)

DNS (record already exists):
- Added allow_overwrite = true to cloudflare_record.ui_a so Terraform takes
  ownership of a pre-existing A record instead of failing

Deprecated field:
- cloudflare_record: value → content (field was deprecated in favour of content)
